askNext: How are images and pages chunked and captioned?
What is end-to-end latency with large media?
How do you cite non-text evidence for users?
multimodal-rag.body: Multimodal RAG expands the library. Libraries still need catalogues and librarians.
multimodal-rag.example: "Multimodal RAG retrieved the right diagram and the model still described last year's version."
multimodal-rag.misuse: Assuming a text RAG pipeline plus a vision model is enough. Chunking, alignment, OCR quality, and latency dominate outcomes.
multimodal-rag.practicalMeaning: Separate indexes per modality when needed, invest in parsing, and evaluate with multimodal questions, not text-only benchmarks.

translations.label: technical meaning
translations.text: Multimodal RAG retrieves and conditions generation on non-text or mixed modalities—images, audio, video, PDFs—using embeddings, captions, or specialised indexes alongside text retrieval.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: Separate indexes per modality when needed, invest in parsing, and evaluate with multimodal questions, not text-only benchmarks.

multimodal-rag.whyExists: Enterprise knowledge is not only markdown. Products needed retrieval that matches how evidence actually exists.

askNext: Is this about interface design, product strategy, or model capability?
Does the vision depend on trust, habit, or lock-in?
Are we building a workflow tool, a companion, or a polite way to blur the line?
mustafa-suleyman.body: It is easier to mock the companion idea than to notice how many major products are inching toward it anyway.
mustafa-suleyman.example: "The roadmap had gone full Mustafa: less tool, more companion, with enough memory and polish to make dependence sound like convenience."
mustafa-suleyman.misuse: By treating "companion" language as either obviously profound or obviously absurd. It is better understood as a serious product thesis about persistence, personalisation, convenience, and software that wants to sit between the user and everything else.
mustafa-suleyman.practicalMeaning: When people invoke Mustafa Suleyman, they usually mean AI as a persistent product layer: more assistant, less query box; more relationship, less transaction.

translations.label: product meaning
translations.text: A sign that the discussion is moving from model performance to user relationship design.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A shorthand for the thesis that AI should feel less like software you visit and more like software that stays with you.

mustafa-suleyman.whyExists: Because AI is not only a research contest. It is also a packaging contest, an interface contest, and a trust contest. Suleyman's name often marks the point where the conversation shifts from raw capability to how intelligence is turned into habit.
